LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CHRISTINE BELL, TRUSTEE OF THE BELL LIVING TRUST, DAVID BELL, MARY BELL, TRUSTEE OF THE 1988 BELL DECLARATION OF TRUST, AND SHARON BELL'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION (NEGLIGENCE) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST SEPARATE OUT THE NEGLIGENT HIRING CAUSE OF ACTION FROM THE OTHER NEGLIGENCE CLAIM. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 3RD - 6TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST SPECIFICALLY PLEAD THE SPECIFICS OF THE CONTRACT OR ATTACHED THE CONTRACT TO THE COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION (NUISANCE) IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS HAD A PREVIOUS OPPORTUNITY TO ADEQUATELY AMEND THIS CLAIM BUT HAS FAILED TO DO SO. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 9TH AND 10TH CAUSES OF ACTION ARE SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS BEEN PROVIDED OPPORTUNITIES TO SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE FACTS TO SUPPORT ITS CLAIMS OF MALICE OR BAD FAITH AND HAS FAILED TO DO SO. THE COURT GRANTED PLAINTIFF LEAVE TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT AS TO THESE CAUSES OF ACTION, AND THE 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION, WHICH WAS NOT SUBJECT TO THIS DEMURRER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.

Appearance:
Ryan Vlasak, Esq., from Bracamontes & Vlasak, 415-835-6777, for plaintiff and moving party
Michael Smith, Esq., from Manning & Kass, 415-217-6990, for opposing party: Cornely Company

After hearing, tentative ruling is adopted.
Plaintiff Jeeba Abbeyquaye'S Motion To Compel Depositions And Inspection Against Defendant. The motion to compel is granted. Defendant shall produce Eric Haynes, Greg Cazatt, Troy MacDonald, Chris Gonzales, and William Judge for deposition by April 6, 2012.
Defendant shall allow the inspection of its computer system which will be limited to seeking the service order and invoice referred to in the February 26, 2010 timesheet. The search shall be limited to 230 Central Avenue, San Francisco location. A protective order shall be prepared to protect the concerns related to trade secret rights, proprietary information rights, privacy rights, as well as information protected from disclosure by attorney-client or product and litigation privileges as well as a premature disclosure of expert discovery information. The search will be limited to 4 hours to minimize the burden in unreasonably interfering with the Defendant's operation of its business.
Request for sanctions is Denied. Order signed in open court. Pro Tem Judge: Arlene Barton, not reported.

Defendant's Demurrer to the 3rd - 6th causes of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must specifically plead the specifics of the contract or attached the contract to the complaint.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 8th cause of action (nuisance) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has had a previous opportunity to adequately amend this claim but has failed to do so.
Defendant's Demurrer to the 9th and 10th causes of action are sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has been provided opportunities to sufficiently allege facts to support its claims of malice or bad faith and has failed to do so. The court granted plaintiff leave to amend the complaint as to these causes of action, and the 11th cause of action, which was not subject to this demurrer. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
